Title: Search bar jump
Rating: 1 Stars

STEPS: 
When searching inside a playlist (pull down from top) the initial search bar shows one line down from the header. 
BUT On tap it jumps one line above…. 😡
Often with a delay…leading to a second tap to initiate search. 
ISSUE: Results in accidentally selecting the first search result in the list 😞
Ruins your currently playing playlist selecting the wrong track on the new playlist. Ruining playing song for audience. 

Solutions:
- Search shows in the top line on pull down (same line as back)
- Or search stays on the second line (same place) the whole time. 
- Or start search result list one line down so one can’t select the first track by accident. 
- fix delay. Delay has been improved (worse on older devices) but still not good when searching your playlists to queue tracks for an audience.

Title: Question of the Week: Ultimate World Cup Anthem?

Hey everyone, The World Cup is finally here, bringing the world together for an incredible month of football. But let's be honest, the action on the pitch is only half the magic. The other half is the unforgettable soundtrack that goes with it. Music has this unique power to freeze a moment in time. Just hearing a few chords of a certain song can instantly transport us back to a legendary goal, a dramatic penalty shootout, or a celebration with friends in front of the TV. Over the years, we’ve been blessed with some iconic official anthems that became global phenomena, like Shakira’s "Waka Waka," K’naan’s "Wavin’ Flag," or Ricky Martin’s "The Cup of Life." However, we know that sometimes the songs that define a tournament for you aren't the official ones at all. It could be a famous fan chant, a massive summer radio hit, or just a personal track that gets you hyped for the match. To kick things off, my absolute favorite is "País do Futebol" by MC Guimê. Even though it wasn't a global official theme, its energy, rhythm, and vibe capture the pure passion and joy of Brazilian football perfectly. It never fails to get me hyped for the games! So, let's open the floor:Which song is the ultimate World Cup anthem for you? It can be a legendary official tournament track or any other song that completely captures that football magic for you! Drop your favorite track in the comments below, tell us why it's your top pick, and share any special football memories attached to it. Can't wait to see what you have! Title: Artist and Genre Algorithm needs improvement

Dear Spotify Team,I have been a Spotify Premium subscriber for many years and have always appreciated the platform's ability to help me discover new music and artists. Unfortunately, over the past several months, I've noticed a significant decline in that experience.Many of the playlists and recommendations being presented to me now feel repetitive and stale. Different playlists often contain many of the same songs, resulting in very little actual music discovery. Instead of being introduced to new artists or tracks, I find myself hearing the same selections over and over again.Additionally, the genres and artists being recommended increasingly do not align with my listening habits. Rather than helping me explore new music within the genres I enjoy, it often suggests music that has little relevance to my tastes.One of the primary reasons I subscribe to a music streaming service is to discover new music. Lately, that aspect of Spotify feels largely absent, and the overall listening experience has become predictable and, frankly, boring.At the same time, subscription prices continue to increase. As a paying customer, I expect ongoing improvements to the service, particularly in areas like personalization and music discovery. Instead, I feel the experience has moved in the opposite direction.I wanted to provide this feedback because I genuinely enjoy Spotify and would prefer to remain a customer. However, if the recommendation and discovery experience continues to decline, I will seriously consider exploring alternative streaming platforms that do a better job of introducing listeners to new music.Thank you for taking the time to read my feedback. I hope it can be shared with the teams responsible for recommendations, playlist curation, and user experience.

1. Hi, If you'd like to share your playlist with your contacts, you can first copy the link to the playlist from Spotify, and then paste the link in a text message, email, social media post, etc: Desktop computer instructions: Right-click on the playlist you’d like to share, or select the three dots next to it Choose Share, and then select Copy link to playlist In the conversation with your contact(s), press Ctrl+V (on a Windows device) or command+V (on a Mac device) to paste the copied playlist link Mobile device or tablet instructions: Open your playlist and tap the three dots to open the menu Choose Share and then select Copy link In the conversation with your contact(s), tap the screen once so that the “Paste” option appears, and then tap it to paste the copied playlist link Feel free to read more about sharing links from Spotify in this support article. If you'd rather prefer to set your playlist to Collaborative, so that other Spotify users whom you invite can add, remove, and reorder tracks in the playlist, you can instead follow these steps to invite them as collaborators: Desktop computer instructions: Right-click on the playlist you’d like to share, or select the three dots next to it Select Invite collaborators to generate a link that’s valid for 7 days In the conversation with your contact(s), press Ctrl+V (on a Windows device) or command+V (on a Mac device) to paste the invite link Mobile device or tablet instructions: Open your playlist and tap the three dots to open the menu Select Invite collaborators to generate a link that’s valid for 7 days In the conversation with your contact(s), tap the screen once so that the “Paste” option appears, and then tap it to paste the invite link
